title: allium campanulatum var. bidwelliae
intro: Allium campanulatum var. bidwelliae, also known as Bidwell's onion, is a rare and endemic species of wild garlic native to the Klamath Mountains in the southwestern part of Oregon, USA. This perennial plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Allium campanulatum var. bidwelliae include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and has also been traditionally used in poultices and salves for topical applications.
